humanSummary.text: RealLive detector lands cleanly across all four acceptance criteria. The detector deterministically distinguishes RealLive from Siglus and AVG32 on the declared fixtures (covered by rejects_reallive_when_siglus_scene_pck_co_present_with_ambiguous_engine_variant_error, rejects_reallive_when_gameexe_dat_co_present_with_ambiguous_engine_variant_error, rejects_reallive_on_avg32_pdt_layout_with_unsupported_engine_variant_error, does_not_detect_reallive_on_siglus_only_fixture_without_misclassifying, and does_not_detect_reallive_on_xp3_fixture_without_misclassifying). Detection is pure Rust: no Command::new appears in crates/kaifuu-engine-fixture/ or in the new detect_reallive matrix row in crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs (the only Command::new hit in kaifuu-core is the pre-existing helper-registry runner at line 7380, not detector code). Ambiguous and unsupported states emit kaifuu.ambiguous_engine_variant and kaifuu.unsupported_engine_variant respectively, with detected=false; envelope-invalid and named-marker-only states emit kaifuu.unknown_engine_variant. rlvm is referenced only as a research anchor: zero matches for `rlvm` across Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock/crates/*/Cargo.toml; all source mentions live in clean-room provenance comments (module-level lib.rs, inline block above RealLiveProfileDetectorAdapter, detect_reallive row comment, and docs/kaifuu-adapters/reallive.md worker checklist). Capabilities report ships Detection/ProfileGeneration/AssetListing/AssetInventory as Supported and every other capability (Extraction, Patching, ContainerAccess, CryptoAccess, CodecAccess, PatchBack, KeyProfile, RuntimeVm, EncryptedInput, AssetTextPatching, DeltaPatching, NonTextSurfaceExtraction) as Unsupported. Worker deviations are all defensible: (1) readiness record at docs/kaifuu-adapters/reallive.md matches plan section 6 explicit recommendation; (2) matrix row uses UnknownVariant for ambiguous/unsupported diagnostic signals, consistent with the BGI/Ethornell pattern of using UnknownVariant when shape is identified but variant cannot be positively classified; (3) golden updates to KAIFUU-095 (xp3-unknown) and KAIFUU-rpg-maker public-fixture detection reports are strictly additive (only a new kaifuu.reallive adapter row with detected=false appears; no pre-existing row content changed; manifest hashes refreshed). Verification: cargo test -p kaifuu-engine-fixture: 54 passed / 0 failed; cargo test -p kaifuu-core: 1 lib + 2 doctest passed / 0 failed; cargo test -p kaifuu-cli: 78 passed / 0 failed; just check: full workspace clean (cargo fmt --check, cargo check --workspace, tsc --noEmit suites all green). One P2 follow-up is emitted for the worker-flagged ALPHA-006 live-corpus calibration concern (very-early RealLive titles that omit #GAMEEXE_VERSION). The readiness record mention is informational; a discrete DAG node is the proper instrument for surfacing concrete real-game false-negative evidence after ALPHA-006 ships, because such evidence may require signal-catalog adjustments that should not silently re-enter under the KAIFUU-172 banner.

findings.title: RealLive detector live-corpus calibration follow-up
findings.description: The detector's positive-identification leg requires at least one of {#GAMEEXE_VERSION, #REGNAME, #G00*, #KOE*, #SEEN*} to appear in Gameexe.ini alongside a valid SEEN.TXT envelope. The worker and readiness record both note that very-early RealLive titles (Kanon-era) may omit #GAMEEXE_VERSION and ship only #REGNAME plus .g00 corroborators, which the current key catalogue does cover but has not been validated against retail bytes. This is an expected and accepted ALPHA-006 calibration concern, not a defect: KAIFUU-172 deliberately shipped without retail bytes per the plan-of-record decision to defer false-negative surfacing to ALPHA-006. A dedicated follow-up node lets ALPHA-006 surface concrete evidence (real game directories that the detector misses) and feeds any signal-catalog adjustment back through a discrete spec rather than silently re-entering the KAIFUU-172 scope.

evidence.detail: REALLIVE_GAMEEXE_INI_KEY_PREFIXES enumerates eleven public-archaeology key prefixes; all are derived from Haeleth's RLDEV site and observed in synthetic fixtures only. No retail RealLive Gameexe.ini bytes were exercised at the KAIFUU-172 slice.
Readiness record explicitly calls out 'Real-game false-negative discovery for very early RealLive titles (Kanon-era ...) — reopened as a new node only after ALPHA-006 surfaces concrete evidence.'
Plan-of-record decision #1 explicitly chose to ship the signal catalog as-is and let ALPHA-006 surface false negatives. The follow-up node operationalizes that decision.

findings.impact: Without a tracked follow-up, any real-game false-negative discovered at ALPHA-006 risks either silent re-entry into KAIFUU-172 (loss of audit trail) or being lost in alpha-vertical triage. A discrete node provides the correct scoping boundary and forces evidence-first calibration changes to land under their own spec.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: ALPHA-006 vertical adapter runs the KAIFUU-172 detector against the Sweetie HD Remaster + Sweets fandisc directories and a small calibration cohort of additional owned RealLive titles spanning the Kanon-era through Little Busters-era release window.

Any title that fails to detect (detected=false despite being a genuine RealLive layout) is captured as a redacted aggregate evidence note (title label + which signals were observed and which were missing); no retail bytes leave private-local.

If at least one title fails on the absence of #GAMEEXE_VERSION while presenting #REGNAME and .g00 corroborators, the follow-up extends the FSM's positive-identification leg to accept #REGNAME + at least one corroborator (.g00 count > 0 or .ovk/.koe/.nwk count > 0) without requiring #GAMEEXE_VERSION, gated on no Siglus markers and no .PDT presence.

Synthetic fixture suite is extended with at least one new positive case mirroring the early-RealLive layout; the regression test asserts detected=true on the new fixture and continues to assert no false positive on the existing negative cases.
Readiness record updates the 'Reference implementations and docs' and 'Real-game false-negative discovery' lines with the concrete evidence collected at ALPHA-006.

proposedDagNode.title: RealLive detector live-corpus calibration follow-up
proposedDagNode.summary: Calibrate the KAIFUU-172 RealLive detector against real-game corpora once ALPHA-006 lands. Use redacted aggregate evidence from owned RealLive titles to surface any false-negative classes the synthetic fixture suite cannot cover (notably early titles that omit #GAMEEXE_VERSION), then land the signal-catalog adjustments under this discrete spec rather than reopening KAIFUU-172.
deliverables: ALPHA-006 vertical adapter invocation of the KAIFUU-172 detector against the alpha-set retail RealLive directories.
Aggregate evidence log (title labels + observed/missing signals only; no retail bytes) captured under the kaifuu engine-research private-local namespace.
If evidence justifies it: an FSM extension and one or more new synthetic fixtures covering the discovered false-negative class, plus regression assertions that the existing negatives still reject.
Updates to docs/kaifuu-adapters/reallive.md reflecting the calibration outcomes and the resolution of the 'real-game false-negative discovery' open item.
acceptanceCriteria: The KAIFUU-172 detector is exercised against the ALPHA-006 alpha-set RealLive directories without shelling out, vendoring rlvm, or relaxing the clean-room posture.
Every detected false-negative class is captured as redacted aggregate evidence (title label + signal presence/absence) before any FSM change is proposed.
Any FSM extension preserves the existing Siglus-overlap and AVG32-lineage rejections; synthetic regression tests assert no misclassification reappears.
Readiness record at docs/kaifuu-adapters/reallive.md closes the 'real-game false-negative discovery' open item or restates it with concrete evidence.

verification.value: Review the redacted aggregate-evidence log for the ALPHA-006 alpha-set RealLive titles and confirm any FSM extension is justified by per-title evidence.

auditFocus: Confirm no rlvm code, headers, or Cargo dependency was added during calibration; clean-room posture preserved.
Confirm aggregate-evidence log contains only redacted labels and signal presence/absence rows; no retail bytes leak.
Confirm Siglus-overlap and AVG32-lineage rejections still hold against the updated FSM via the existing synthetic negatives.
Confirm any new synthetic positive fixture explicitly models the evidence class (e.g. #REGNAME + .g00 corroborator, no #GAMEEXE_VERSION) that justified the FSM extension.
